item.title: FLASHPOINT! PROTEST PHOTOGRAPHY IN PRINT READING ROOM On view March 12 - May 31,...
item.description:
@printedmatterinc

FLASHPOINT! PROTEST PHOTOGRAPHY IN PRINT READING ROOM
On view March 12 - May 31, 2026
‼️Join us for the opening reception March 12, 6-8 PM.

Printed Matter presents Flashpoint!, a traveling reading room exhibition organized by 10×10 Photobooks dedicated to protest photography in print. The presentation brings together a selection of photobooks, zines, posters, pamphlets, independent journals, and alternative newspapers from the 1950s to the present.

Across these formats, photography emerges as both a tool of protest and a cultural artifact of resistance, capturing political struggles as they unfold and as they are later remembered.

Upcoming events:

Protest and Resistance Posters Panel Discussion at Printed Matter with Adrian Franks, Arthur Fournier and Daylon Orr Thursday, 2 April, 6 pm

History Repeats Itself: Curating Protest Panel Discussion at the New York Public Library Moderated by Deirdre Donohue with Maggie Mustard and La Tanya Autry
Wednesday, 13 May, 6 pm

The presentation is based on Flashpoint! Protest Photography in Print, 1950-Present, an anthology published by 10×10 Photobooks in 2024, edited by Russet Lederman and Olga Yatskevich (New York City: 10×10 Photobooks, 2024). Publications available for purchase.

@10x10photobooks
item.content:encoded:
@printedmatterinc

FLASHPOINT! PROTEST PHOTOGRAPHY IN PRINT READING ROOM
On view March 12 - May 31, 2026
‼️Join us for the opening reception March 12, 6-8 PM.

Printed Matter presents Flashpoint!, a traveling reading room exhibition organized by 10×10 Photobooks dedicated to protest photography in print. The presentation brings together a selection of photobooks, zines, posters, pamphlets, independent journals, and alternative newspapers from the 1950s to the present.

Across these formats, photography emerges as both a tool of protest and a cultural artifact of resistance, capturing political struggles as they unfold and as they are later remembered.

Upcoming events:

Protest and Resistance Posters Panel Discussion at Printed Matter with Adrian Franks, Arthur Fournier and Daylon Orr Thursday, 2 April, 6 pm

History Repeats Itself: Curating Protest Panel Discussion at the New York Public Library Moderated by Deirdre Donohue with Maggie Mustard and La Tanya Autry
Wednesday, 13 May, 6 pm

The presentation is based on Flashpoint! Protest Photography in Print, 1950-Present, an anthology published by 10×10 Photobooks in 2024, edited by Russet Lederman and Olga Yatskevich (New York City: 10×10 Photobooks, 2024). Publications available for purchase.

@10x10photobooks

item.title: Announcing the 2026 NY Art Book Fair! Apply now, via pmabf.org or the link in bi...
item.description:
@printedmatterinc

Announcing the 2026 NY Art Book Fair! Apply now, via pmabf.org or the link in bio, through Monday, March 30, 2026 at 11:59pm EST. 💗💓💗

NYABF 2026 Fair identity by Draw Down Books @drawdownbooks

#NYABF2026

@printedmatterinc

Announcing the 2026 NY Art Book Fair! Apply now, via pmabf.org or the link in bio, through Monday, March 30, 2026 at 11:59pm EST. 💗💓💗

NYABF 2026 Fair identity by Draw Down Books @drawdownbooks

#NYABF2026
item.author: printedmatterinc

item.title: ✨ Celebrate 50 Years of Printed Matter with Guest of Honor Ed Ruscha! 🥂 April 21...
item.description:
@printedmatterinc

✨ Celebrate 50 Years of Printed Matter with Guest of Honor Ed Ruscha!
🥂 April 21, 2026

Join us in celebrating 50 years of Artwork in Bookform—50 years of book launches and artist talks; ever-expanding audiences for creative expression; exhibitions, publications, and window installations by artists; serendipitous discovery among the stacks; finding community and countercultural voices within zines, books, artists’ editions, and ephemera … and so much more! Since 1976, Printed Matter has championed generations of artists working outside commercial and algorithmic systems, creating space for experimentation, access, and community to come first.

Our 50th Anniversary Benefit brings together some of the most dynamic and inspiring voices in contemporary art and publishing, including our Guest of Honor Ed Ruscha, introduced by Christophe Cherix; a Founder Spotlight on Pat Steir; and an Artist Spotlight on BlackMass Publishing.

Celebrate Printed Matter’s legacy and invest in our future at a moment when independent culture and artists' voices are under increasing pressure. In addition to being a celebratory moment, this Benefit Dinner is also a critical means of raising funds for Printed Matter's essential work in the field of artists' books and publishing. Individual tickets start at $1,500; Benefit Committee support begins at $3,000 and includes recognition in key event materials, a Contributor-level Membership (to commence upon the launch of our newly restructured Membership Program this spring!), and at least two seats or a table at the dinner.

🌟 Click the link in our bio for more information and to reserve tickets or join the Benefit Committee! 🌟

Can’t be there in person? You can still sustain our work by joining the Benefit Committee from afar, or by making a tax-deductible donation via the link in bio.

From all of us at Printed Matter—thank you for being part of this story!

@edruschaofficial
@patsteir
@blackmasspublishing
item.content:encoded:
@printedmatterinc

✨ Celebrate 50 Years of Printed Matter with Guest of Honor Ed Ruscha!
🥂 April 21, 2026

Join us in celebrating 50 years of Artwork in Bookform—50 years of book launches and artist talks; ever-expanding audiences for creative expression; exhibitions, publications, and window installations by artists; serendipitous discovery among the stacks; finding community and countercultural voices within zines, books, artists’ editions, and ephemera … and so much more! Since 1976, Printed Matter has championed generations of artists working outside commercial and algorithmic systems, creating space for experimentation, access, and community to come first.

Our 50th Anniversary Benefit brings together some of the most dynamic and inspiring voices in contemporary art and publishing, including our Guest of Honor Ed Ruscha, introduced by Christophe Cherix; a Founder Spotlight on Pat Steir; and an Artist Spotlight on BlackMass Publishing.

Celebrate Printed Matter’s legacy and invest in our future at a moment when independent culture and artists' voices are under increasing pressure. In addition to being a celebratory moment, this Benefit Dinner is also a critical means of raising funds for Printed Matter's essential work in the field of artists' books and publishing. Individual tickets start at $1,500; Benefit Committee support begins at $3,000 and includes recognition in key event materials, a Contributor-level Membership (to commence upon the launch of our newly restructured Membership Program this spring!), and at least two seats or a table at the dinner.

🌟 Click the link in our bio for more information and to reserve tickets or join the Benefit Committee! 🌟

Can’t be there in person? You can still sustain our work by joining the Benefit Committee from afar, or by making a tax-deductible donation via the link in bio.

From all of us at Printed Matter—thank you for being part of this story!

@edruschaofficial
@patsteir
@blackmasspublishing
